config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena tanpa perlindungan hak pribadi, “kepentingan masyarakat” mudah dipakai sebagai alasan untuk menekan kelompok kecil atau orang yang tidak populer. Saya paham pentingnya kepentingan bersama, apalagi untuk keamanan dan ketertiban, tetapi itu seharusnya dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan dasar individu. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dihormati, bukan dari individu yang selalu dikalahkan demi mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society matter, but they should not automatically outrank the rights of the individual. From my American perspective, I am wary of any vague appeal to "society" because it can quickly become a tool for trampling unpopular people, minority views, or basic freedoms. At the same time, I do not think individual rights are absolute in every case; laws for public safety, fraud prevention, and disease control show that some limits are necessary. The key is that society should justify those limits very carefully, not assume it has the right to override individual liberty whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan tekanan pada orang kecil, minoritas, atau mereka yang suaranya tidak kuat. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi dalam kondisi tertentu seperti keamanan publik atau kesehatan masyarakat, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan tidak boleh berubah jadi kebiasaan untuk mengorbankan kebebasan dasar. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru lahir ketika hak pribadi dihormati dulu, lalu kepentingan bersama dibangun di atas fondasi itu, bukan sebaliknya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my American perspective, that kind of slogan is too easy to abuse, because "society" often means the preferences of whoever has power at the moment, not some neutral common good. I do accept that rights can be limited for real public necessities like safety, disease control, and preventing fraud, but those limits need to be narrow, evidence-based, and accountable. If we start from the idea that society comes first, it becomes much easier to excuse censorship, discrimination, and pressure on unpopular people.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan yang lemah, minoritas, atau orang yang tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi tanpa batas hak individu yang jelas, istilah itu mudah sekali berubah jadi alat pembenar untuk sensor, diskriminasi, dan pemaksaan. Saya setuju hak bisa dibatasi dalam keadaan tertentu seperti keamanan publik atau kesehatan, tetapi pembatasannya harus ketat, terukur, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Justru masyarakat yang sehat lahir ketika hak individu dihormati dulu, lalu kepentingan bersama dibangun di atasnya, bukan dengan mengorbankan orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. In the United States, we have seen how broad claims about the "common good" can become excuses for censorship, discrimination, or forcing unpopular people to conform, so I am very skeptical of that framing. That said, I do believe some rights can be limited for concrete public needs like safety, disease control, and fraud prevention, but only with narrow, evidence-based rules and real accountability. For me, the burden should always be on society to justify any limit on liberty, not on the individual to prove why their rights deserve protection.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
